name: Braun Series 9 Pro 9477cc Wet & Dry Electric Shaver with 5 Sync Elements
whatWorks: The 5-sync shaving elements work together in a way that no 3-element shaver can replicate — the ProLift Trimmer specifically targets flat-lying hairs that the foils can't catch, then cuts them before they reset. The result is noticeably fewer missed hairs and a closer shave on the first pass than the Series 7.

HyperLift technology lifts hairs up from the skin surface before cutting, which matters most for people with fine, flat, or short hair that lies flush against the skin. This is the specific reason dermatologists sometimes recommend the Series 9 for sensitive skin — the hair gets lifted and cut without the foil dragging against skin.

SkinGuard pressure sensor stops the motor from producing skin-irritating micro-cuts if you press too hard. Electric shaver beginners consistently apply too much pressure, and this safety net makes the learning curve significantly gentler.

Four weeks of use from a single 60-minute charge is accurate for most users shaving once daily. The battery life improvement over the Series 7 (3 weeks) reflects a larger cell, and you get a quick-charge feature that provides enough power for a single shave in 5 minutes.
worthKnowing: At $399 CAD, this is an investment that needs to be weighed against a lifetime of cartridge razor purchases. The math typically favors the Series 9 after about 2 years of use, but only if you actually commit to the electric shaver long-term — many people return to blades within 3 months.

The Pro Cleaning & Charge Station uses the same replacement cartridges as the Series 7 at similar cost. Braun has standardized this across the lineup, which is convenient but the cost remains $12–18 CAD per cartridge for regular users.

The 5-element head is noticeably larger than the Series 7 and some other shavers, which can make it slightly harder to navigate tight areas like under the nose. Some users prefer the Series 7's more compact head for this reason.

Braun recommends replacing the 5-element cassette every 18 months. The Series 9 Pro replacement cassette runs $60–80 CAD — higher than Series 7 due to the additional elements. Set a calendar reminder so you're not surprised.

commonComplaints.title: Very expensive for what it is…
commonComplaints.body: I can't tell the difference between this and the Series 7 I replaced. Both shave well. I'm not sure the extra $150 was worth it for me personally.

commonComplaints.title: Replacement heads are costly…
commonComplaints.body: The shaver performs great but the replacement cassette was $70. I expected that going in but worth flagging for anyone budgeting.

commonComplaints.title: Large head is hard near the nose…
commonComplaints.body: The 5-element head is wider than my old shaver. I have to work harder to get clean lines under my nose compared to a smaller head shaver.

commonComplaints.title: Not noticeably closer than my old shaver…
commonComplaints.body: I came from a Series 7 and honestly my morning shave feels the same. The Pro Lift is a nice idea but I can't feel the difference in practice.

verdict: The Braun Series 9 Pro is the best foil electric shaver most people can buy — the 5-sync element system and HyperLift technology genuinely capture more hair in fewer passes than any 3-element competitor. For sensitive-skin users or anyone who's struggled with electric shavers missing flat-lying hairs, it's the clear recommendation. The $399 CAD price is objectively high and the Series 7 closes 80% of the performance gap at $150 less — but for the user who wants the absolute best electric shave and will keep it for 5+ years, the math works.

realBuyers.quote: I've had electric shavers for 15 years. This is the first one that actually gets as close as a blade on my face. The 5 elements make a real difference.
My skin used to get so irritated from shaving. This with the SkinGuard has been a complete turnaround. I can shave every day without any redness.
Worth the premium over the Series 7. The ProLift catches the hairs on my chin that were always a problem area with every other shaver I've tried.
Three months in and my skin has never been better. The cleaning station handles everything and the shave quality hasn't dropped at all.

sources.title: Braun Series 9 Pro Review — Gear Patrol

metaDescription: Braun Series 9 Pro electric shaver review for Canada: 5-sync elements, HyperLift, SkinGuard. Top-tier foil shave. ~$399 CAD on Amazon.ca.
introText: The Braun Series 9 Pro 9477cc is Braun's flagship foil electric shaver, featuring 5 synchronized shaving elements including the ProLift Trimmer for lifting flat-lying hairs and SkinGuard pressure protection. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B099TK24M5) at around $399 CAD.
